label: Eco-Skills Core v2.0 (Gemini)
note: Gemini gemini-2.5-flash ingestion of Book+Manual → 60 proposals → reviewed (9 dup rejected) → 51 blocks accepted, evidence-rich, principles auto-created. ESS/Syllabus/Website pending (rate limit).

structured.description: Training single-pointed concentration and presence — gathering scattered attention back to a calm centre.
Systematic practice of sustaining and returning attention.
procedure: Focus on the breath or a single object
Gently return when the mind wanders
research: Focused-attention meditation improves sustained attention performance

structured.description: The Eco-Skills Score (ESS) — a questionnaire-based assessment measuring a person's current state across the Eco-Skills dimensions, used to personalise the programme.
A structured set of questions across breath, light, water, nutrition, thought, awareness, focus and personality.
structured.purpose: To establish a baseline and track progress.

structured.description: How raw responses convert to dimension scores and an overall Eco-Skills Score, with interpretation bands.
structured.purpose: To translate answers into actionable guidance.

structured.description: Integrating body, mind, emotion and values into a whole, balanced personality and character.
Aligning daily action with ecological and ethical values.
structured.purpose: To build integrity and a sense of meaning.
research: Values-based living is linked to wellbeing in self-determination research

structured.description: Nurturing warm relationships and community — a key driver of oxytocin and long-term health.
structured.purpose: To meet the human need for belonging.
research: Social connection is among the strongest predictors of longevity (Holt-Lunstad 2010)

structured.description: Cultivating a calm, positive mind so the body releases serotonin, dopamine, oxytocin and endorphins rather than chronic cortisol and adrenaline.
Honouring the body's nightly repair cycle as the foundation of mental and physical health.
structured.purpose: To allow the brain and body to consolidate, clear and restore.
procedure: Avoid stimulants late in the day
research: Sleep deprivation increases next-day amygdala reactivity by ~60% (2007)

structured.description: Understanding and lowering the chronic stress response that floods the body with cortisol and adrenaline.
structured.purpose: To move from a fight-or-flight state to rest-and-repair.
research: Chronic cortisol elevation is associated with impaired memory and immunity

structured.description: The disciplined cultivation of positive, present-centred thinking — managing the fifth channel: the thoughts we hold.
structured.purpose: To tip the body's chemistry toward repair by reducing rumination and stress thoughts.
procedure: Notice negative thought loops without judgement
Anchor attention in the present moment
research: Cognitive reappraisal reduces amygdala reactivity in neuroimaging studies

structured.description: Three weeks to turn the core Eco-Skills into stable daily habits.
21-Day · Week 1 — Physical Channels
21-Day · Week 2 — Mind & Awareness
21-Day · Week 3 — Focus & Personality
A one-week introduction that builds the core daily Eco-Skills habits, one channel at a time.
Orientation & baseline ESS
Eco-Respiration foundation
Eco-Light & morning rhythm
Eco-Hydration & Nutrition
Eco-Thought basics
Mindful Observation
Integration & reflection
A full ninety-day transformation across all five channels and four dimensions, matched to the 3-month syllabus.
90-Day · Month 1 — Foundation
90-Day · Month 2 — Deepening
90-Day · Month 3 — Integration
Caring for the body through the four physical channels — breath, water, light and nutrition — so pure, oxygen-rich blood circulates freely and the body releases its restorative chemistry.
Mindful intake of clean water in tune with the body's rhythms, recognising that every cell reaction depends on water.
structured.purpose: To keep cells, blood and detoxification pathways functioning optimally.
procedure: Begin the day with a glass of warm water
Sip throughout the day rather than gulping
research: Even mild dehydration (1–2%) impairs cognition and mood

structured.description: Conscious daily exposure to natural light — especially morning sunlight — to anchor circadian rhythm and mood.
structured.purpose: To regulate the body clock, sleep and vitamin D.
research: Morning light exposure advances circadian phase and improves sleep onset

structured.description: Eating natural, seasonal, plant-rich food with awareness and rhythm — food as information for the body's chemistry.
structured.purpose: To supply the body with living nutrients and stabilise energy and mood.
research: Diet quality is linked to lower risk of depression in large cohort studies

structured.description: Eco-Respiration is the conscious practice of slow, deep, rhythmic breathing that maximises oxygen exchange and activates the parasympathetic nervous system. It is the first and most powerful Eco-Skill because breath is the only autonomic function we can also control at will.
structured.purpose: To restore full, diaphragmatic breathing so the body shifts out of chronic stress and into repair, improving oxygenation of blood and calming the mind.
benefits: Increases oxygen supply to brain and tissues
Activates the parasympathetic 'rest and repair' response
Improves focus, sleep and emotional regulation
procedure: Sit comfortably with a straight spine in fresh air or near greenery
Exhale fully through the nose, drawing the navel inward
Inhale slowly for a count of 4, expanding the belly first then the chest
Hold gently for a count of 2
Exhale for a count of 6, longer than the inhale
Repeat for 5–10 minutes, ideally at sunrise
research: Slow breathing (~6 breaths/min) increases heart-rate variability, a marker of parasympathetic tone
Controlled breathing has been shown to reduce salivary cortisol in randomized studies
Diaphragmatic breathing improves sustained attention and reduces negative affect (2017)

structured.description: Eco-Skills are life-skills based on ecological principles — the conscious daily management of the five natural channels (breath, light, water, nutrition and thought) to improve physical & mental health, self-awareness, concentration and holistic personality. Developed by Prof. B. D. Tripathi, BHU.
Developing the capacity to observe one's own body, breath, emotions and thoughts — the inner foundation of all Eco-Skills.
The skill of recognising, naming and understanding one's emotions.
structured.purpose: To respond wisely to feelings rather than be driven by them.

structured.description: Non-judgemental attention to present-moment experience.
structured.purpose: To create the inner space needed to choose responses rather than react.
procedure: Pause and notice breath and body
research: Mindfulness training increases prefrontal regulation of emotion

knowledge.statement: Mental health is defined as a state of emotional, psychological, and social well-being where an individual realizes their potential, copes with life's stresses, works productively, thinks rationally, acts effectively, and contributes to their community.

evidence.text: It is defined as a state of emotional, psychological and social well-being in which every individual realizes his or her own potential, can cope with normal stresses of life, can work productively, think rationally and act effectively and is able to make a contribution to their community.

knowledge.statement: Eligibility for the Eco-skills training course requires passing 10+2 with a minimum of 50% marks.

evidence.text: Eligibility:This training course shall be available to those who have passed 10+2 with a minimum of 50% marks.

knowledge.statement: Mental Health is characterized by a stress-free, energetic, and positive thinking state of the human mind.

evidence.text: Mental Health is stress free, energetic and positive thinking of human mind.

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills practices, based on ecological principles, improve various life domains, focusing on five major dimensions: Physical Health, Mental Health, Self-awareness, Eco-Centralization, and Holistic Personality.

evidence.text: Eco skills practices based on ecological principles helps to improve various domains of life with its major areas of work on five dimensions namely Physical Health, Mental Health, Self-awareness, Eco-Centralization and Holistic Personality.

knowledge.statement: Responses for the ESS are obtained on a three-point format: 'Yes', 'Sometimes', and 'No'.

evidence.text: The responses were to be obtained on a three point response format, ‘Yes’, ‘Sometimes’ and ‘No’.

knowledge.statement: Air, Water, and Soil are considered the basic structural components for the formation of the human body within the Biosphere.

evidence.text: In the Biosphere, Air, Water and Soil are treated as the basic structural components for the formation of the human body.

knowledge.statement: Changes in the natural properties of abiotic components (Air, Water, Soil) will lead to changes in the structural, functional, and developmental behavior of human beings.

evidence.text: If the natural properties of these abiotic components are changed it will certainly lead to a change in the structural, functional & developmental behavior of human beings.

knowledge.statement: Ecology is defined as the study of how organisms interact with one another and with their physical environment.

evidence.text: Ecology is the study of how organisms interact with one another and with their physical environment.

knowledge.statement: Self-Awareness involves having a clear perception of one's personality, strengths, unique skills, weaknesses, thoughts, relationship with nature, beliefs, emotions, and motivation to achieve ultimate goals.

evidence.text: Self-Awareness – It is about having a clear perception of one’s personality, strengths & unique skills, weaknesses, thoughts, relationship with nature, beliefs, emotions and motivation of being able to achieve one’s ultimate goal.

knowledge.statement: The preliminary 57-item ESS was tried out on a sample of 400 subjects from diverse socio-cultural backgrounds, ages (18-45 years), and genders, including both rural and urban populations.

evidence.text: The preliminary format of ESS with 57 items were tried out by administering it on a sample of 400 subjects belonging to various socio-cultural background, age and gender.
The samples were being extracted from the population age ranging from 18-45 years constituting both the males and females with their rural and urban background.

knowledge.statement: The ESS covers all 5 dimensions of Eco-skills: Physical Health, Mental Health, Self-Awareness, Eco-centralization, and Holistic Personality Development.

evidence.text: This scale covers the 5 dimensions of Eco-skills:
Physical Health
Mental Health
Self-Awareness
Eco-centralization
Holistic Personality Development

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills Scale (ESS) is an assessment tool that measures acquired life skills based on ecological principles using a three-point response format.

evidence.text: Eco-skills Scale (ESS) measures the acquired life skills based on ecological principles on a three point response format.

knowledge.statement: The techniques introduced through Eco-skills are derived from making sense of careful observations of natural phenomena.

evidence.text: The technique introduced through Eco-skills are based on making sense out of the careful observations of the natural phenomena.

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills improve the counseling, teaching, and mentoring abilities of teachers.

evidence.text: Eco-skills improve counseling, teaching and mentoring ability of the teachers.

knowledge.statement: Physical health can be affected by human biology (genetics and physiology) and environmental factors (exposure to sunlight or toxic substances).

evidence.text: Physical health is critical for overall well-being, and can be affected by :
Human biology: A person’s genetics and physiology.
Environment: Exposure to factors such as exposure to sunlight or toxic substances.

knowledge.statement: Based on significance, 50 out of 57 items were selected to form the final version of the ESS scale.

evidence.text: On the basis of the significance 50 items out of 57, were selected to constitute the final form of scale.

knowledge.statement: The Eco-skills Scale (ESS) is a standardized tool developed by subject experts using appropriate statistical methods to measure acquired life skills based on ecological principles, utilizing a three-point response format.

evidence.text: Eco-skills Scale (ESS) to measures the acquired life skills based on ecological principles on a three-point responses format which has been developed with the help of subject experts and standardized as per norms using appropriate statistical methods.

knowledge.statement: Items for each dimension of the ESS were worded both positively and negatively.

evidence.text: Items of each dimension were worded positively and some were negatively worded.

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills are scientific in nature.

evidence.text: Eco-skills are scientific in nature.

knowledge.statement: The ESS provides a total of six scores, including a composite Eco-skills performance score and individual scores for each of the five dimensions.

evidence.text: The scale provides a total of six Scores.
It comprises a composite of the Eco-skills performance score as well as the scores of each five dimensions it covers.

knowledge.statement: Ecological principles such as Adaptation, Behavior, Diversity, Energy flow, Growth and Development, and Limits and Regulation can explain the origin, growth, behavior, development of life, and its interactions with nature on Earth.

evidence.text: The origin, growth, behavior and development of life and its interactions with nature on earth can be explained with the help of Ecological principles (Adaptation, Behavior, Diversity, Energy flow, Growth and Development, Limits and Regulation etc.).

knowledge.statement: Items for the ESS were selected after careful review and discussion with subject experts, and were made meaningful in the Indian context.

evidence.text: Selection of the items was made after a careful review and discussion with subject experts.
Items of the scale were made more meaningful in Indian context.

knowledge.statement: The effects of Eco-skills practice were tested on a sample of 400 individuals, comprising both males and females aged 18-45 years.

evidence.text: The effects of practice have been tested on 400 samples constituting both males and females of age group 18-45 years.

knowledge.statement: Individual performance on the Eco-skills Scale is assessed and nominally categorized into four categories: Severe Borderline Persona, Moderate Borderline Persona, Enhanced Persona, and Actualized Persona.

evidence.text: Eco-skills Scale Performance of the individual is assessed and categorized nominally into four categories (i) Severe Borderline Persona (ii) Moderate Borderline Persona (iii) Enhanced Persona and (iv) Actualized Persona with the help of Eco-skills scale.

knowledge.statement: Life on Earth originated ecologically through billions of reactions among Air, Water, and Soil in the presence of the Sun's electromagnetic radiations.

evidence.text: Ecologically, life on earth originated with billions of reactions among the Air, Water and Soil in presence of electromagnetic radiations of the Sun.

knowledge.statement: The fundamental beliefs and attitudes regarding the functioning of Eco-skills are rooted in the nature and environment of 'Earth' and the lessons derived from it.

evidence.text: The basic beliefs and attitudes about how Eco-skills work are based on the nature and environment of the 'Earth' and the lessons that can be learned from it.

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills enhance an individual's physical and mental health, memory power, eco-centralization, self-awareness, working efficiency, and immunity, while also reducing mind stress.

evidence.text: Eco-skills improve physical & mental health, memory power, eco-centralization, self awareness, working efficiency, immunity and reduces the mind stress.

knowledge.statement: The administration of the ESS must be completed within a 30-minute time limit.

evidence.text: Administration of ESS has to be completed within 30 minutes, which is the time limit assigned for the test.

knowledge.statement: Physical health can be briefly defined as the uninterrupted circulation of pure and oxygenated blood in the human body.

evidence.text: In brief, physical health can be defined as the uninterrupted circulation of pure and oxygenated blood in the human body.

knowledge.statement: Mental health can be summarized as the stress-free, energetic, and rational thinking of the human mind.

evidence.text: Hence mental health can be summarized as the stress free, energetic and rational thinking of human mind.

knowledge.statement: Holistic personality is enhanced through eco-skills by improving communication skills, time management, leadership skills, working behavior, and self-confidence.

evidence.text: Holistic personality is enhanced by communication skills, time management, leadership skills, working behavior and self confidence through eco-skills.

knowledge.statement: A total of 82 items were initially constructed for the ESS, with specific distributions across the five dimensions (15 for Physical Health, 14 for Mental Health, 18 for Self-Awareness, 16 for Eco-centralization, and 19 for Holistic Personality Development).

evidence.text: A total of 82 items (15, 14, 18, 16, 19 for the above five dimensions respectively) were constructed for the scale.

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills contribute to the development of empathy, harmony, and peace within society.

evidence.text: Eco-skills develop empathy, harmony & peace in the society.

knowledge.statement: The Eco-skills training course has a total duration of 120 hours, with specific allocations for different modules: Physical & Mental Health (30 hrs), Self Awareness (20 hrs), Eco-Centralization (20 hrs), and Holistic Personality Development (50 hrs).

evidence.text: Duration:120 hrs Actual Training (Physical & Mental Health-30 hrs; Self Awareness-20 hrs; Eco-Centralization-20 hr; Holistic Personality Development-50 hrs)

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills are life skills grounded in ecological principles that enhance Physical & Mental Health, Self-Awareness, Eco-centralization, and Holistic Development of one's personality.

evidence.text: Eco-skills are the life skills based on ecological principles which improve Physical & Mental Health, Self-Awareness, Eco-centralization and Holistic Development of one’s personality.

knowledge.statement: Eco-centralization is the ability to achieve one's goals by improving mind concentration through critical observations, deep desires, greater dedication, and strong focus on actions.

evidence.text: Eco-centralization – It deals with the ability to achieve one’s goal by improving mind concentration through critical observations, deep desires, greater dedication towards work and strong focus on his actions.

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills are applicable at every stage of life.

evidence.text: It is applicable at every stage of life.

knowledge.statement: Higher overall scores on the ESS indicate an extremely satisfactory and highly stable personality.

evidence.text: Higher overall scores of ESS are indicative of extreme satisfactory and highly stable personality.

knowledge.statement: After scrutiny, 25 items with similar meanings were dropped from the ESS.

evidence.text: As a result, after scrutiny 25 such items were dropped which seemed to hold the similar meaning.

knowledge.statement: The instructions on the ESS test form are sufficient to guide respondents through the statements.

evidence.text: The instructions on the test form are sufficient to take care of statements that are given.

knowledge.statement: Human behavior and working efficiency can be controlled by the proper management of Air, Water, and Soil related components of the human body and the radiant energy of the Sun.

evidence.text: Therefore, human behavior and their working efficiency may be controlled by the proper management of Air, Water and Soil related components of the human body and radiant energy of the Sun.

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills are scientific in nature and their underlying beliefs and attitudes are based on observations of Earth's nature and environment.

evidence.text: Eco-skills are scientific in nature.
The basic beliefs and attitudes about how Eco-skills do and view its work are based on the nature and environment of the Earth and what can be learned from it.

knowledge.statement: The distribution and abundance of organisms on Earth are influenced by the reciprocal relationship between abiotic components (air, water, soil) and biotic components (plants and animals).

evidence.text: The distribution and abundance of organisms on earth is shaped by reciprocal relationship of abiotic (air, water and soil) and biotic (plants and animals) components.

knowledge.statement: Experts rated the face validity of ESS items on a three-point scale (yes, sometimes, no), with scores of 2, 1, and 0 for positively worded items and reversed for negatively worded items.

evidence.text: Expert rated the face validity of the items on a three point scale (yes, sometimes & no).
These ratings were given a score of 2, 1 and 0 respectively on positive worded and the reverse on the negative worded.

knowledge.statement: The maximum possible score for the ESS is 100, and the minimum is zero.

evidence.text: The maximum possible score for ESS is 100 and the minimum is zero.

knowledge.statement: Holistic Personality Development focuses on an individual's intellectual, emotional, social & communication skills, physical, artistic, creative, spiritual, and leadership potentials, and defines personality based on time management.

evidence.text: Holistic Personality Development – It focusses on the person’s intellectual, emotional, social & communication skills, physical, artistic, creative, spiritual and leadership potentials.
It defines individual’s personality as a whole on the basis of time management too.

knowledge.statement: A significance of correlation at 0.01 was established as the criterion for retaining items in the ESS.

evidence.text: The significance of correlation at 0.01 was fixed as the criterion for retaining the items.

knowledge.statement: Eco-Centralization is an ecological technique aimed at achieving one's goals by enhancing mind concentration through critical observations, deep desires, greater dedication, and strong focus.

evidence.text: Eco-Centralization is an ecological technique to achieve one's goal by improving mind concentration through critical observations, deep desires, greater dedication and strong focus.

knowledge.statement: Physical Health is defined as the condition of one's body, encompassing everything from the absence of disease to fitness level, and is critical for overall well-being.

evidence.text: Physical Health – It is the condition of one’s body, taking into consideration everything from the absence of disease to fitness level.
Physical health is critical for overall well-being.

knowledge.statement: The sample for the study was randomly selected from rural, urban, and sub-urban areas of Varanasi and surrounding districts.

evidence.text: The sample was being selected randomly from rural, urban and sub-urban areas of Varanasi and surrounding districts.

knowledge.statement: Eco-skills foster holistic personality development and confidence among students for self-regulation, utilizing the concepts of 'Energy Flow' and 'Eco-centralization'.

evidence.text: Eco-skills develop holistic personality & confidence among students for self regulation through the concepts of 'Energy Flow' & 'Eco-centralization’.
